resources:
UI System Hardware Network Storage Android Security IPC AI / ML Dynamic Delivery
Demo Shutdown Demo FileExplore Demo Service Demo Animation Demo Modern Permission Demo Job Service Demo Floating Alert Dialog Demo Floating Alert Window Demo Bluetooth Demo Notification Demo Share Provide Action Demo Hello JNI Demo Hello JNI (Legacy) Demo Flash Light Demo Device Admin Demo Camera Demo Video Record Demo Alarm Demo Binder Call Demo Scheduler Service Demo Table Layout Demo Database Demo Room Database Demo System UI Demo Work Manager Demo Work Manager Legacy 1 Demo Work Manager Legacy 2 System UI Integration Lab This lab demonstrates 5 core pillars of Quick Setting Tiles & App Widgets. These components integrate your app directly into the Android System UI for high-frequency interactions.
1. Instant Toggle & Feedback One-tap actions (like Flashlight) with haptic feedback and immediate state change.
2. Real-time Status Awareness Dynamic labels and subtitles that update based on background app state without opening the UI.
3. Deep Link Shortcuts Instant navigation to specific app features, collapsing the notification shade automatically.
4. Non-Intrusive Interaction Modify settings via system dialogs that appear over the shade, keeping users in their current task.
5. Secure Lock-Screen Access Demonstrates different behaviors when the device is locked vs unlocked (e.g., requiring PIN for sensitive actions).
System Integration Actions Request Add Tile (API 33+) Pin Home Widget (API 26+)
Toggle & Status Tile Configuration Dialog Tile Deep Link Shortcut Tile Secure Action Tile
Tile successfully added to your system panel! Tile is already in your Quick Settings panel. Tile not added (Result Code: %1$d) Request Add Tile is only available on Android 13+ (API 33). Widget pin request sent to Home Screen. Pinning widgets is only available on Android 8.0+ (API 26). Device is locked! Please verify identity to perform this action. Please unlock the device to change settings via dialog. Tile state updated via dialog. Authentication successful! Performing secure action... Secure Action Complete. Tile updated: %1$s Launch Result Back to Demo Pin App Widget Demo Material Widget Demo Handler Thread Demo XML Parser Demo Wi-Fi Demo Binding Demo Simple Corouting
Koltlin Demo Retrofit Corouting
Koltlin "Demo BLE " Demo Compose UI Demo State/Shared Flow Demo Page 3 Demo DataStore Demo DataStore Koltlin Demo Encryption Database Demo Encryption DB Military-Grade DB Protection 1. Field-Level Encryption (Standard) Only specific columns are encrypted using Android Keystore. The DB file itself is readable, but sensitive content is junk. Full Database Encryption: The entire .db file is encrypted using SQLCipher. Without the key, even the table names are invisible. Please enter all fields Item saved Alias (Keystore) Sensitive Data Save (Field Enc) Alias (SQLCipher) Secret Info Save (Full DB Enc) Alias: Encrypted (in DB): Decrypted (Safe): Full DB Alias: Secret Data: (This info is only visible because DB is OPENED with correct key)
Encrypted SharedPreferences Compare Normal vs Encrypted storage. Even if someone steals your XML file, they can't read encrypted data. Setting Key (e.g. user_token) Setting Value Save Pair Load Key Clear All Standard XML Storage: (Stored as plain text in /shared_prefs/) Decrypted (Safe View): (Auto-decrypted via Keystore) Raw Ciphertext (XML file content): (This is what hackers see in the XML file) Current Load Results for key: "%1$s" Error initializing: %1$s Please enter key and value Please enter a key to load Saved successfully! All preferences cleared
Demo USB Device Refresh USB No USB device connected All Files Access is required for this demo All Files Access Required

Welcome to quick setting demo. Info: Material Switch
Please input name Go to welcome page Welcome, %1$s！ please input nonempty string Demo view binding and press next button to go to the next activity Exit Demo data binding: You can see that the text view is changed by edit the name field. Input User ID load user data loading... This demo uses Kotlin coroutines with suspend and async to load user data asynchronously. Tap the button below to start. data from source 1 data from source 2 Result1: %1$s 
Result2: %2$s Enter a user ID and press Load Data.
This demonstrates Retrofit + Coroutine using suspend and async under the hood. User ID Load Data Waiting for result... User: %1$s (%2$s) Error: %1$s Unpair BT Pair BT Connect... BT is enable BT is disable BT adapter is valid... Messenger binder call AIDL binder call AIDL is configured. Messenger is configured. "I got the data from UI: " The result is overflow!!! Go to Setting Need notification permission We need notification permissions to remind you of important events, such as reminders, alerts, or background task completion. Please manually enable notification permissions in Settings. Need Overlay window permission We need this permission to display prompts or control panels. Please go to the settings page to enable it. System dialog Click the button to dismiss dialog Scan start Stop BLE Scan Try to connect: %1$s Lack of connection permissions Connect success: %1$s Connect failed: %1$s BONDED BONDING NONE Unknown Name: %1$s
 MAC address: %2$s
 State: %3$s Ble device infomation Bt is not supported Alarm count: %1$d Please config offset time first InValid input offset time!!! No this alarm function!!! Please input the value is large than 60 sec!!! Please input the value is large than 15 min!!! No file!!! Service is bound. Service is unBind. Service is not Bind yet. Game Start!!! You win! Game ended in a tie! Computer Win！ Loading... All permissions granted Some permissions denied Work task is Active Work task is Idle Time is arrived 2024 OCT 24 This is note content example Create note Save Update Note Update Cancel
 Option: Delete
Technical Instruction This demo demonstrates the Content Provider and Content Resolver mechanism. Content Providers manage access to a central repository of data, allowing applications to securely share data with other apps. Content Resolvers act as an interface to communicate with providers using URIs. This demo showcases the Room Persistence Library. Room provides an abstraction layer over SQLite to allow fluent database access while harnessing the full power of SQLite. It features DAOs (Data Access Objects) for clean separation of concerns and compile-time verification of SQL queries. Service Monitor Remote service mode local service mode Please ensure that administrator privileges have been granted in the system settings. Click the button below to start the image blurring test. This is a custom GifView component that demonstrates high-performance GIF animation playback and control. It leverages the modern AnimatedImageDrawable and ImageDecoder (API 28+) to provide smoother rendering and better memory management compared to the legacy Canvas/Movie approach. Function instruction: This page shows how to launch the "system-level" floating dialog box. After launching, the app will automatically close after 3 seconds, while the dialog box will remain on top of the screen until you tap "OK". Interaction Demo This is an advanced floating window demo. Once launched, a circular view appears in the center of the screen. You can change its position by "long-pressing and moving". This demonstrates the combined application of WindowManager and a custom Touch Strategy. Function instruction: This demo implements two background camera control modes: 1. JobIntentService: for immediate hardware operations. 2. WorkManager (Jetpack): for ensuring reliable task execution and observing state changes (observer pattern). Front lens Rear lens Camera2 API Video Streaming and Shooting Demonstration Please record first!!! Share Action Demo System share mechanism This page demonstrates the implementation of ShareActionProvider. It encapsulates plain text content using Intent.ACTION_SEND and automatically retrieves a list of applications within the system that can receive this data format. Please click the share icon on the ActionBar to test. "JNI info: %1$s" JNI Core Mechanism Demo This page demonstrates how Android calls low-level C++ libraries via the NDK. It includes: 
1. Java calling Native methods; 
2. Native posting data back to the Instance via reflection; 
3. Native updating Static (Class) variables. Waiting for native instructions... JNI function Call (Java -> Native) Obj Callback Static Callback Object Callnack Static Callback JNI Demo Monitor
 Callback Log Clear Object Clear Class Binder IPC Demo AIDL (Direct) Messenger (Queue) Input A input B (IPC Log): Send Binder Request
Compose Service Monitor Jetpack Compose Practice (Advanced) • Unidirectional Data Flow (UDF) for Service lifecycle
• Optimized LazyColumn with stable keys
• Adaptive Layout (BoxWithConstraints) for cross-device support
• Material 3 Dynamic Color & custom animation indicators Logs Quick Controls
Service Running: Bound:
TRUE FALSE
Start Stop Bind Unbind Send
Service Started Service Stopped Service Bound Service Unbound Command Sent Service Status Controls
Modern Flow Lab Event Logs Counter: %1$d Instant message from SharedFlow! Time: %1$s Increment counter Trigger toast event Clear log Toast Event Received: %1$s HandlerThread Tech Demo This section demonstrates the core application of Android HandlerThread. By establishing a Looper in the background thread, asynchronous task processing is achieved and data is safely sent back to the UI thread. Background task progress count "" Times low light mode Hide interface Current status: Standard mode Current status: Immersive mode (full screen) Current status: Low brightness mode" Notification function demonstration This section demonstrates the core mechanisms of the Android notification system, including Channel creation, large image style updates, and PendingIntent navigation logic. This is crucial for medical alerts or system status notifications. You have successfully accessed this page by clicking the notification. This proves that the navigation logic of PendingIntent and TaskStackBuilder is working correctly. Notification updated This demonstrates how to dynamically modify the content of a sent notification. Info: test This is Demo text Device Admin & Security Demo This module demonstrates DevicePolicyManager vs BiometricPrompt: 

1. Device Admin: High-privilege control (Force Lock). Requires manual activation in System Settings. 
2. Security Lock: Direct call to KeyguardManager for PIN/Pattern/Password validation. 

Note: BiometricPrompt (see other demo) is the modern way to combine Biometrics + Device Credentials. Please verify your identity to continue. This demo demonstrates several triggering mechanisms of AlarmManager: • Repeat: Strictly periodic alarm (requires > 60s) • Inexact: System-optimized power-saving mode (requires > 15min) • AllowWhileIdle: Can be triggered even in Doze mode XML Pull Parser Demo Parse XML resources using State Patterns and dynamically generate data lists. MVVM + Chain of Responsibility This demo demonstrates AI chess-playing logic implemented using the Chain of Responsibility pattern, and achieves automated UI refresh and winning path highlighting through Data Binding and ViewModel. The game begins! M3 Components Demo SYSTEM PARAMETER CONTROL Interactive Components The following components demonstrate how to drive the ViewModel and record system behavior through Data Binding. Toggle System State Single Selection Mode Debug Mode Release Mode Multi-select filters (Filter Chips) Sensor Data BLE Status Network CLEAR LOG System UI control panel This demo demonstrates how to use the WindowInsetsController API to control the display, hiding, and immersive behavior of the status bar and navigation bar. This is crucial for medical image viewing or full-screen game interfaces. The demo showcases WindowInsetsController controls. Content is adapted to the safe area to avoid being obscured by the system navigation bar. Animation display mode Confirmed username Please enter your name here to experience synchronization. The text below will update in real time as you type. Share content instantly Static (Class) Instance (Object) Value A Value B Retrofit + Coroutine Instruction:
- StateFlow (State): UI updates on value change.
- SharedFlow (Event): One-time actions (Toast/Snack).
- RecyclerView Log: High-performance event tracking. Increment Trigger Event Paging 3 Demo: 1. Simulates 2000ms network latency 2. Automatic pagination loading (Page Size: 20) 3. Automatic state retention after screen rotation (cachedIn) Refresh List start to play gif!!! stop to play gif!!! Error: %1$s DataStore Demo: Asynchronous transaction processing with support for MVVM auto-response. Current storage state summary Mode: %1$s | Lang: %2$s | Font: %3$.2f Dark Light Enable Dark Mode Preview Font Size Preview (Slider): %1$.2f Interface language control Chinese English Operation logs (real-time flow monitoring): Demo DataStore (Koltlin) Demo Navigation UI Home Page Settings This is home fragment Data updated! Time: %1$s Demo Navigation Architecture Navigation Core Concepts: • NavHostFragment: The container that holds the page. • NavController: The controller that manages page navigation. • NavGraph: An XML map that defines destinations. • NavigationUI: Automatically connects NavControllers and UI elements. 🏗️ System Architecture Implementation: • MVVM: Separates logic from UI, improving testability. • Data Binding: Direct communication between XML and ViewModel LiveData. • View Binding: Type-safe, replacing the traditional findViewById. Refresh data Home Settings Navigation DataStore Demo Turn on notification permissions Reset DataStore Return to home page Offset must be greater than 0 Invalid input number Unknown strategy selected
Running... Waiting for start... This module demonstrates background schedule services:
• Periodic Trigger: Set interval via SeekBar (s).
• Status Monitoring: Real-time task timing.
• System Integration: Send Notification when time arrives.
• Auto Termination: Manual stop or activity destroy.
Modern Permission Model Demo This demo demonstrates the modern way to handle runtime permissions using the ActivityResult API. It includes single and multiple permission requests, handling rationales, and navigating to settings for permanently denied permissions. Request Camera Permission Request Camera & Microphone Check Permission Status Permission Status: %1$s Permission granted: %1$s Permission denied: %1$s Permission permanently denied. Please enable it in settings. Camera permission is required to take photos in this demo.
Demo TensorFlow Lite AI / ML: Image Classification This demo showcases TensorFlow Lite integration with MVVM. It uses the TFLite Task Library to classify images from the gallery. Results include the object category and confidence score. Select Image Classification Result: TFLite model not initialized. No objects detected. Error loading image.
Demo CameraX (Modern) CameraX Architecture This demo demonstrates Jetpack CameraX. It features: 
• Lifecycle Awareness 
• Preview & Image Capture 
• Real-time Image Analysis 
• Simplified device compatibility. Capture View Image Camera State: %1$s Camera permission is required to use CameraX. Captured image file not found. It may have been deleted or not saved correctly. No image captured yet! Capture Done!!! Image saved successfully!
Open camera error result: %1$d Preview configuration failed Record session failed Already recording Recording stopped No such file Recording... Start recording Play video Exit page
Demo ML Kit Vision Dynamic Feature Modules Dynamic Feature Demo
Biometric Authentication Biometric authentication is available. No biometric features available on this device. Biometric features are currently unavailable. The user hasn't associated any biometric credentials. A security update is required. Biometric status unknown. Unexpected error checking biometric availability. Biometric login for DemoSet Log in using your biometric credential Authentication error: %1$s Authentication succeeded! Authentication failed Instruction: This demo demonstrates BiometricPrompt API, providing a unified system dialog for fingerprint, face, and iris authentication. It automatically handles hardware availability and security fallback to device credentials (PIN/Pattern/Password). Authenticate
On-Device Vision (ML Kit) Real-time Barcode Scanning, Face Detection, and Text Recognition. All processing happens on-device for privacy and speed. Barcode Face Text Barcode: %1$s Face detected! Text: %1$s Mode switched. Ready... Error: %1$s Point camera at target
On-Demand Feature Delivery This demo demonstrates Dynamic Delivery using Google Play Feature Delivery. It allows downloading and installing features on-demand, significantly reducing initial APK size. Note: Requires Play Store environment for real downloads, but can be tested with "bundletool" locally. Install Feature Launch Feature Uninstall (Deferred) Module Status: %1$s
Pending Downloading Installing Installed Not Installed Failed (%1$d) Canceled Unknown (%1$d) Module installed successfully! Installation failed with error code: %1$d API Not Available (-5): Deferred Uninstall requires a real Google Play Store environment and is not supported in local testing mode. Action failed: %1$s Uninstallation requested. It will happen in the background.
Connected successfully Connect to %s Connection failed: %1$s Connecting (Legacy mode)... Permission is granted Permission is not granted WiFi state disabled WiFi state enabled Network disconnected Network connected Network connecting No WiFi found Connected Scanning WiFi, please wait... Connecting WiFi, please wait... Disconnecting WiFi, please wait... Disconnect Are you sure you want to disconnect? Scan result: %1$s
LeakCanary Integration App Startup Optimization Benchmarking Lab Technical Description (Instruction) Core Technology: Test Scenario: Expected Result: Execute: Enter %1$s Test Page
LeakCanary 2.x, Heap Dump Simulate Activity destruction while held by a static strong reference. Leak notification triggered within 5 seconds after returning to main screen. LeakCanary Integration Simulate Memory Leak Manual Watch (No Leak) Object is now being watched by AppWatcher Clear History Current Status: %1$s Leak Reports: Class: %1$s Path: %1$s Size: %1$d bytes No reports yet. Click the button above and wait for LeakCanary analysis. Analysis in Progress LeakCanary is analyzing the heap dump. This may take a few seconds...
Jetpack Startup, Coroutines Compare Synchronous Blocking vs. Asynchronous Optimized Startup. Demonstrate reduced startup time and main thread release. App Startup Optimization Test the impact of different startup strategies on performance. Sync Blocking Startup Async Optimized Startup Executing... Significant jank detected! Smooth startup flow Total Duration: %1$d ms Tasks Completed: %1$d
Jetpack Microbenchmark, Frame Metrics Algorithm performance and UI scrolling jank testing. Generate quantitative reports and comparison charts. Benchmarking Performance benchmarking and quantitative analysis. 1. Micro-benchmark: Algorithm Comparison Run Algorithm Benchmark 2. Macro-benchmark: UI Frame Metrics Monitoring Start UI Tracking Stop and Generate Report Scenario: %1$s Type: %1$s Execution Time: %1$.2f ms Median Frame Time: %1$.2f ms Jank Count: %1$d Jank Rate: %1$.2f%%
Power Management Demo Instruction This module demonstrates various power actions. 

1. Shell Commands: Requires Root (su) for most actions.
2. System Intent: Requires the app to be a System App or have specific permissions (android.permission.SHUTDOWN). Status: %1$s Waiting... Device has Root access. Device does NOT have Root access. Standard Reboot Power Off (Shell) Power Off (System Intent) Basic Actions Advanced Reboot Options (Root) Reboot to Recovery Reboot to Bootloader Soft Reboot (Zygote) Other Utilities Turn Off Screen (Power Key) Attempting Reboot... Attempting Shutdown... Attempting Reboot to Recovery... Attempting Reboot to Bootloader... Attempting Soft Reboot (Restart Zygote)... Attempting to turn off screen... Requesting Shutdown via Intent... Command success. Command failed (Exit: %1$d). Root required. Error: %1$s System Intent failed: android.permission.SHUTDOWN required. (System App only)
Advanced Graphics Graphics Engine & Shaders This module demonstrates the evolution of Android graphics from 2D Canvas to 3D hardware acceleration. 

1. 2D Canvas: Path animation and linear shaders. 
2. 3D Engine (OpenGL/Vulkan): Hardware-accelerated rendering using GLSurfaceView and programmable pipelines. 

Switch effects to compare rendering performance and techniques. Toggle Animation Switch Effect Wave Effect (2D) Spiral Effect (2D) 3D Cube (GL ES 3.0) Say Hello Calc (5+7) Sys Info Bluetooth state ON Bluetooth state OFF Service status: %1$s Permissions denied Permission denied
